perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all solely at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (ii) requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Proprietary Information may be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) prevents the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all solely at the discretion of both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, or (ii) requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Proprietary Information may be disclosed, provided the Receiving Party agrees to certain key performance indicators. The KPIs can be determined by the Disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the disclosure of Proprietary Information. Initially, the Disclosing Party had sole discretion to disclose. Now, disclosure depends on both parties' discretion, and potentially on the Receiving Party's acceptance of KPIs. This contradicts the original clause that the disclosure depends on the disclosing party and the Receiving Party has to meet KPI's. This contradicts clause 3 Return of Materials. Immediately upon (i) the decision by either ------------------- party not to enter into a relationship as a result of the exchange of information hereunder, or (ii) a request by the Disclosing Party at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Return of Materials. Immediately upon (i) the decision by either ------------------- party not to enter into a relationship as a result of the exchange of information hereunder, or (ii) a request by the Disclosing Party at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extracts or derivatives thereof to the extent it is requested by either party in writing, except that a single copy may be retained for legal archival purposes, subject to protection and non-disclosure in accordance with the term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions for disclosing proprietary information, initially at the Disclosing Party's sole option, to require agreement from both parties and potentially the Receiving Party meeting KPIs. This contradicts the 'Return of Materials' clause (3), which states that the Receiving Party must return Proprietary Information upon either party's decision not to enter a relationship or at the Disclosing Party's request, implying the Disclosing Party's unilateral control over the information's return, which is now undermined by the need for both parties' agreement for initial disclosure as defined in the change text. The change creates an ambiguity as to when the receiving party has to return materials.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may, at its discretion, notify the Disclosing Party of any potential unauthorized release or other potential breach of which it is aware, conditional on whether the Receiving Party believes the breach will harm the Disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandated immediate written notification of any unauthorized release or breach. The modified version makes notification discretionary and dependent on the Receiving Party's assessment of harm. This is an in-text contradiction, because it makes the decision of breach completely up to the Receiving Party which can affect remedies. This contradicts clause 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder, that any such breach may allow the Receiving Party or third parties to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party resulting in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, and therefore, that upon any such breach or any threat thereof, the Disclosing Party may seek appropriate equitable relief (without the need to post bond or other security) in addition to whatever remedies it might have at law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder, that any such breach may allow the Receiving Party or third parties to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party resulting in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, and therefore, that upon any such breach or any threat thereof, the Disclosing Party may seek appropriate equitable relief (without the need to post bond or other security) in addition to whatever remedies it might have at law. The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware. In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be to any extent illegal, invalid or unenforceable, such provisions shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that this Agreement shall otherwise remain in full force and effect. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to principles of conflicts of law. The parties agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or validity of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for the County of San Diego, California, and each party hereby agrees to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such courts. This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended except in an express writing signed by officers of both parties. No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of such party and no failure or delay in enforcing any right will be deemed a waiver. Each party warrants to the other that it is duly authorized to enter into this Agreement and that the terms of this Agreement are not inconsistent with any of its respective outstanding contractual obligations. The execution and performance of this Agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into any other agreement or to perform any obligations other than as specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated immediate written notification of any unauthorized release or breach. The changed version makes notification discretionary, depending on the Receiving Party's assessment of harm to the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, specifically the sentence 'The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware.' The modification introduces ambiguity and a conflict of interest as it shifts the obligation to notify from a mandatory action to a discretionary one, which affects the Disclosing Party's right to seek equitable relief as stated in clause 6.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to principles of conflicts of law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of law, unless the Receiving Party is headquartered in California, in which case California law will govern.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conditional governing law. The original agreement specifies California law. The modified version introduces Delaware law unless the Receiving Party is based in California, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over which law applies. This will affect clause 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder, that any such breach may allow the Receiving Party or third parties to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party resulting in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, and therefore, that upon any such breach or any threat thereof, the Disclosing Party may seek appropriate equitable relief (without the need to post bond or other security) in addition to whatever remedies it might have at law. Where remedies may be different in each state.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder, that any such breach may allow the Receiving Party or third parties to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party resulting in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, and therefore, that upon any such breach or any threat thereof, the Disclosing Party may seek appropriate equitable relief (without the need to post bond or other security) in addition to whatever remedies it might have at law. The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware. In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be to any extent illegal, invalid or unenforceable, such provisions shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that this Agreement shall otherwise remain in full force and effect. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to principles of conflicts of law. The parties agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or validity of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for the County of San Diego, California, and each party hereby agrees to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such courts. This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended except in an express writing signed by officers of both parties. No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of such party and no failure or delay in enforcing any right will be deemed a waiver. Each party warrants to the other that it is duly authorized to enter into this Agreement and that the terms of this Agreement are not inconsistent with any of its respective outstanding contractual obligations. The execution and performance of this Agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into any other agreement or to perform any obligations other than as specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies California law governs the agreement. The changed text introduces Delaware law unless the Receiving Party is based in California. This directly contradicts the last sentence of section 6 in the Contradicted Text, which states 'This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to principles of conflicts of law.' This creates a clear contradiction as the governing law is now conditional, directly conflicting with the absolute statement of California law in section 6.

perturbation.original_text: The parties shall be entitled to exchange Proprietary Information under the <PAGE> terms of this Agreement for a period not to exceed two (2) years from the date hereof, unless otherwise extended by mutual written agreement of the parties or incorporated into a separate agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties shall be entitled to exchange Proprietary Information under the <PAGE> terms of this Agreement for a period not to exceed one (1) year from the date hereof, unless otherwise extended by mutual verbal agreement of the parties. If this is verbally accepted this must be ratified by written acceptance within 30 calendar days or the agreement is null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the duration for exchanging proprietary information and the way the term extends. Originally, the exchange period was two years, extendable only by written agreement. Now, it's one year, extendable by verbal agreement ratified by written acceptance within 30 days. This affects clause 1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use Obligations. The Receiving Party -------------------------------------- agrees (i) to hold the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information in strict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information as the duration that they need to keep confidentially will be different.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Non-Disclosure and Non-Use Obligations. The Receiving Party -------------------------------------- agrees (i) to hold the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information in strict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its most confidential materials), (ii) not to divulge any such Proprietary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person (except consultants or agents, subject to the conditions stated below), (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally whether to enter into a proposed business transaction with the Disclosing Party without the prior written permission of the disclosing party, (iv) not to remove or export from the United States or re-export any such Proprietary Information or any direct product thereof except in compliance with all licenses and approvals required under applicable export laws and regulations, including without limitation, those of the U.S. Department of Commerce, and (v) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information. Any employee, consultant or agent given access to any such Proprietary Information must have a legitimate "need to know" and shall be similarly bound in writing. The parties shall be entitled to exchange Proprietary Information under the <PAGE> terms of this Agreement for a period not to exceed two (2) years from the date hereof, unless otherwise extended by mutual written agreement of the parties or incorporated into a separate agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the exchange of proprietary information lasts for two years and can only be extended by mutual written agreement. The changed text reduces this period to one year and allows for verbal extensions, which must then be ratified in writing within 30 days. This directly contradicts the 'Non-Disclosure and Non-Use Obligations' section, which references the original two-year period and written agreement. The change affects the duration for which the Receiving Party is obligated to hold the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information in strict confidence and the process for extending that obligation, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the terms are now inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended except in an express writing signed by officers of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties. Any amendments agreed upon via email will be considered binding upon confirmation of receipt of the amendment email.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandated amendments only through express writing signed by officers. The modified version allows amendments via email between authorized representatives, binding upon confirmation of receipt. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the method of amending the agreement, potentially affecting the validity of amendments. This affects clause 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder. The term is vague, meaning one state can accept this, but another state can deny it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Miscellaneous. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that ------------- due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, there can be no adequate remedy at law for any breach of its obligations hereunder, that any such breach may allow the Receiving Party or third parties to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party resulting in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, and therefore, that upon any such breach or any threat thereof, the Disclosing Party may seek appropriate equitable relief (without the need to post bond or other security) in addition to whatever remedies it might have at law. The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware. In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be to any extent illegal, invalid or unenforceable, such provisions shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that this Agreement shall otherwise remain in full force and effect. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to principles of conflicts of law. The parties agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or validity of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for the County of San Diego, California, and each party hereby agrees to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such courts. This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended except in an express writing signed by officers of both parties. No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of such party and no failure or delay in enforcing any right will be deemed a waiver. Each party warrants to the other that it is duly authorized to enter into this Agreement and that the terms of this Agreement are not inconsistent with any of its respective outstanding contractual obligations. The execution and performance of this Agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into any other agreement or to perform any obligations other than as specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments must be in writing and signed by officers, while the changed text allows amendments via email between authorized representatives, binding upon confirmation of receipt. This directly contradicts the method of amendment outlined in the contradicted text of clause 6, which states 'This Agreement may not be amended except in an express writing signed by officers of both parties'. The changed text therefore creates an in-text contradiction.
